info.description: Dashboard system with AI-powered chart generation
info.title: TRVE Dashboards API

post.description: Create a new AI session for chart creation or editing. Creates a chart draft.

get.description: Get an AI session by ID with current chart state

delete.description: Cancel the AI session and discard the draft

post.description: Send a user message to an AI session. The AI agent will process the message asynchronously.
Save the AI session by publishing the draft as a new final version

get.description: Subscribe to real-time updates for an AI session via WebSocket
get.summary: Subscribe to AI session events via WebSocket

get.description: Stream real-time debug events from the AI agent including LLM requests, responses, and tool calls
Get the current status of the AI debug system

post.description: Discover devices on an MQTT connection (e.g., via Zigbee2MQTT bridge)
Executes the command configured on a control component, using either schema-based or legacy template interpolation

400.description: Bad request - not a control or missing connection

post.description: Send a command to a datasource that supports write operations (e.g., stream.websocket-bidir)
post.summary: Execute a command on a bidirectional datasource

400.description: Bad request - connection does not support write

get.description: List device types with optional filtering and pagination

post.description: Create a new device type template

get.description: Get the list of valid device categories
Get the list of valid control UI types (toggle, scalar, button, text, plug, dimmer)
Get a device type by ID

delete.description: Delete a device type by ID

get.description: List devices with optional filtering and pagination

post.description: Create a new device instance
Bulk import devices from discovery

get.description: Get a device by ID
Returns camera names from a Frigate NVR connection
Returns the MP4 clip for a detection event (supports Range headers)
Returns the JPEG snapshot for a detection event
Returns recent detection events for a camera
Returns connection metadata needed for direct WebSocket access (go2rtc MSE)
Proxies the JSMPEG WebSocket stream for a camera through the backend
Returns the WebP thumbnail for a review segment

parameters.description: Camera name (required — encoded into the thumbnail filename)
parameters.name: camera

get.description: Returns Frigate review segments, defaulted to unreviewed alerts. Used by the Frigate Alerts widget.

post.description: Marks one or more Frigate review segments as reviewed (viewed), removing them from unreviewed listings

parameters.description: Review IDs to mark: { ids: string[] }
parameters.name: body

get.description: Returns the latest JPEG snapshot for a camera
Get all available categories with their type counts
Get all registered adapter types with their capabilities and configuration schema
Get details about a specific adapter type including configuration schema
Get all settings that can be modified by administrators
Get a single setting by key

put.description: Update the value of a user-configurable setting

get.description: WebSocket endpoint that pushes server status at specified intervals
get.summary: Subscribe to server status via WebSocket

parameters.description: Update interval (e.g., '5s', '1s'). Default: 5s, Min: 1s, 0 means one-shot
parameters.name: interval

post.description: Validates a user key (GUID) and returns user info if valid

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's ID, name, and capabilities
Get a paginated list of charts (latest version of each) with optional filtering

parameters.description: Filter by component type (chart, control, display)
parameters.name: component_type

post.description: Create a new chart with data source binding and visualization config. Creates as version 1 with status "final".

get.description: Get lightweight chart summaries for card-based selection UI
Get the latest version of a chart by ID

put.description: Update the latest version of a chart in-place (for manual edits)

delete.description: Delete all versions of a chart by ID

get.description: Get the draft version of a chart (if exists)

delete.description: Delete the draft version of a chart (if exists)

get.description: Get version metadata for a chart (version count, has draft, etc.)
Get all versions of a chart by ID
Get a specific version of a chart by ID and version number

delete.description: Delete a specific version of a chart

get.description: Retrieves system-wide configuration including layout dimensions
Retrieves configuration for a specific user

put.description: Updates configuration for a specific user

get.description: Subscribe to a topic and return the first message's schema (columns and sample values)
Subscribe briefly to discover available topics on an MQTT broker
get.summary: Get topics from an MQTT broker

get.description: Get a paginated list of dashboards with optional filtering. Use include_datasources=true to get data source names for each dashboard.

parameters.description: Include data source names from charts
parameters.name: include_datasources

post.description: Create a new dashboard with panels and embedded charts

get.description: Get a dashboard by ID (includes panels and charts)
Retrieve all datasources with pagination and optional type/tag filters

parameters.description: Filter by datasource type (api, websocket, file)
parameters.name: type

parameters.description: Filter by tags (OR semantics, repeat param)
parameters.name: tags

post.description: Create a new data source (API, WebSocket, or File)

get.description: Get statistics about active bucket aggregators
Get a list of all active streaming connections

post.description: Test a datasource connection without saving it

get.description: Retrieve a single datasource by its ID

put.description: Update an existing datasource by ID

get.description: Retrieve all database names from an EdgeLake node's blockchain registry
get.summary: Get databases from an EdgeLake data source

get.description: Retrieve column names and types for a specific table from an EdgeLake node
get.summary: Get table schema from an EdgeLake data source

get.description: Retrieve table names for a specific database from an EdgeLake node
get.summary: Get tables from an EdgeLake data source

post.description: Check the health of a specific datasource and update its status

get.description: Retrieve all possible values for a specific label from a Prometheus datasource
get.summary: Get values for a Prometheus label

post.description: Execute a query and return normalized results
post.summary: Execute a query against a datasource

get.description: Retrieve tables and columns for SQL datasources. Only SQL-type datasources support this endpoint.
get.summary: Get database schema for a SQL datasource

get.description: Opens an SSE connection to stream real-time data from a socket datasource
get.summary: Stream data from a socket datasource

post.description: Opens an SSE connection to stream time-bucketed aggregated data
post.summary: Stream aggregated data from a socket datasource

get.description: Get status information for an active stream
Check if the server and dependencies are healthy

post.description: Process a JSON-RPC message for MCP protocol

get.description: Establish an SSE connection for MCP protocol
Returns a merged list of tags across connections, components, and dashboards with per-entity-type counts
get.summary: List all tags with usage counts

get.description: Returns a paginated list of all users

post.description: Creates a new user account

get.description: Returns a user by their ID

value.description: The value from the control (bool, number, string, or null for buttons)

models.AIMessage.description: A message in the AI conversation

dashboard_id.description: Auto-attach to this dashboard on save

panel_id.description: Auto-attach to this panel on save

data_path.description:
DataPath is the JSON path to the array of records (e.g., "data", "results", "items")
If empty, assumes response is already an array or will be parsed as key-value pairs

models.Chart.description: Chart/Control with data source binding, query config, and visualization settings

chart_type.description: bar, line, pie, gauge, etc. (charts only)

data_mapping.description: How to map data to chart (charts only)

thumbnail.description: Base64 preview image for card display

title.description: Display title (defaults to Name if empty)

use_custom_code.description: Whether custom code mode is enabled

models.ChartDataMapping.description: Mapping configuration from data columns to chart axes/series

aggregation.description: Aggregation to apply (first, last, avg, etc.)

column_aliases.description: Display names for columns (column name -> display name), primarily for dataview

filters.description: Client-side filters applied after data fetch

group_by.description: Column to group/split series by (client-side grouping)

series.description: Column that identifies each series (e.g., "location") - used for time bucket partitioning

x_axis_format.description: Format for X axis values: chart, chart_time, chart_date, chart_datetime, short, long, etc.

models.ChartListResponse.description: Response containing a list of charts with pagination

models.ChartQueryConfig.description: Query configuration for fetching chart data

raw.description: SQL query, filter, or API path

models.ChartSummary.description: Minimal chart info for selection cards and lists

version_count.description: Total versions for this chart id

primary_key.description: Whether column is part of primary key

models.CommandConfig.description: Configuration for sending commands via a connection

models.ControlConfig.description: Configuration for interactive control components (buttons, toggles, etc.)

command_config.description: Legacy command configuration (deprecated, use device type instead)

models.CreateAISessionRequest.description: Request body for creating a new AI session

chart_id.description: Existing chart ID to edit (optional, omit for new chart)

chart_type.description: For charts: bar, line, pie, etc.

component_type.description: Pre-flight context (optional) - sets fields on the draft directly

control_type.description: For controls: button, toggle, slider, etc.

dashboard_id.description: Dashboard panel context (optional) - if set, saved component is auto-attached to this panel

models.CreateChartRequest.description: Request body for creating a new chart or control

models.CreateDashboardRequest.description: Request body for creating a new dashboard

mask_secrets.description: If true, secrets are masked in API responses (default: true)

models.CreateDeviceRequest.description: Request body for creating a new device

models.CreateDeviceTypeRequest.description: Request body for creating a new device type

models.CreateUserRequest.description: Request body for creating a new user

models.Dashboard.description: Dashboard with panels that reference standalone charts

models.DashboardListResponse.description: Response containing a list of dashboards with pagination

models.DashboardPanel.description: Panel position and size in the grid with optional chart reference or text config

models.DashboardSummary.description: Dashboard info with optional data source names for display in tiles

datasource_names.description: Unique data source names used by charts

models.DashboardSummaryListResponse.description: Response containing dashboard summaries with optional data source info

type.description: first, last, min, max, avg, sum, count, limit

op.description: Operator: eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte, contains, in, notIn, isNull, isNotNull

value.description: Value to compare against (can be array for 'in' operator)

mask_secrets.description: If true, secrets are masked in API responses

supported_schemas.description: Control schema support - which control schemas this connection supports

type.description: LEGACY: Keep for backwards compatibility during migration

type_id.description:
NEW: Registry-based type system (preferred)
TypeID format: "category.name" (e.g., "db.postgres", "stream.websocket-bidir")

models.Device.description: A specific device instance with connection and topic bindings

models.DeviceCapability.description: A capability that a device type supports (e.g., state, brightness)

models.DeviceListResponse.description: Response containing a list of devices

models.DeviceType.description: Template that defines a class of IoT device, its capabilities, and command protocol

models.DeviceTypeListResponse.description: Response containing a list of device types

models.DiscoverDevicesResponse.description: Response from device discovery on a connection

models.DiscoveredDevice.description: A device discovered from a bridge (e.g., Zigbee2MQTT)

models.DisplayConfig.description: Configuration for non-chart visual components (cameras, iframes, etc.)

alert_severity.description: Filter by severity: "alert" (default), "detection", or "" for all

default_camera.description: Pre-selected camera name (frigate_camera) or camera filter (frigate_alerts)

frigate_connection_id.description: Frigate-specific fields (used by frigate_camera and frigate_alerts)

max_thumbnails.description: Frigate alerts grid fields (only used when display_type = "frigate_alerts")

models.ImportDevicesRequest.description: Request to bulk import discovered devices

models.PanelTextConfig.description: Configuration for native text panels — section headers, date/time, titles

size.description: Font size in pixels (int) or legacy name (string)

raw.description: Raw query string (SQL, filter expression, etc.)

models.SendMessageRequest.description: Request body for sending a user message

models.SlidingWindow.description: Time window configuration for limiting data to recent entries

duration.description: Window duration in seconds (e.g., 300 = last 5 minutes)

data_path.description:
DataPath is the JSON path to the data payload (e.g., "data", "payload", "message.readings")
Supports dot notation for nested paths. If empty, treats entire message as the data object.

exclude_fields.description: ExcludeFields removes these fields from output

field_mappings.description: FieldMappings renames fields in the output (e.g., {"temp": "temperature", "ts": "timestamp"})

include_fields.description: IncludeFields limits output to only these fields (empty = include all)

timestamp_field.description: TimestampField specifies which field contains the timestamp (default: use server receive time)

timestamp_format.description:
TimestampFormat is the Go time format string for parsing timestamps (default: RFC3339)
Common formats: "2006-01-02T15:04:05Z07:00" (RFC3339), "2006-01-02 15:04:05", unix timestamp

interval_ms.description: Auto-poll interval in milliseconds (0 = manual only)

config_refresh_interval.description: seconds - how often frontend should refresh dashboard/chart config

data_type.description: Store data type: json, schema, text (default: json)

push.description:
Push connection configuration for streaming (ts-store v0.2.2+)
When streaming is enabled, dashboard calls ts-store API to create a push connection
and ts-store dials out to dashboard's inbound WebSocket endpoint

store_name.description: Name of the store to query

agg_default.description:
AggDefault is the default aggregation function for fields not in AggFields
Options: avg, sum, min, max, first, last, count

agg_window.description:
AggWindow is the aggregation window duration (e.g., "1m", "5m", "1h")
When set, records are aggregated over this time window before sending

connection_id.description:
ConnectionID stores the active push connection ID returned by ts-store
This is set when the push connection is created and used to manage/delete it

filter.description: Filter is an optional substring filter - only send matching records

format.description: Format specifies the message format: "full" (default) or "compact" (for schema stores)

from.description: From is the starting timestamp in nanoseconds (0 = oldest data, -1 = current time/realtime only)

id.description: Optional: existing connection ID to resolve masked secrets from DB

models.TimeBucket.description: Time bucket configuration for aggregating streaming data into intervals

function.description: Aggregation function: avg, min, max, sum, count

interval.description: Bucket interval in seconds (e.g., 60 = 1 minute, 3600 = 1 hour)

timestamp_col.description: Column containing timestamps for bucket alignment

models.ToolCall.description: A tool call made by the AI

models.UpdateChartRequest.description: Request body for updating an existing chart, control, or display

models.UpdateDashboardRequest.description: Request body for updating an existing dashboard

mask_secrets.description: If provided, updates secret masking setting

models.UpdateDeviceRequest.description: Request body for updating an existing device

models.UpdateDeviceTypeRequest.description: Request body for updating an existing device type

models.UpdateUserRequest.description: Request body for updating an existing user

models.User.description: User account with authentication and capabilities

models.UserCapabilitiesResponse.description: Current user's capabilities and access permissions

models.UserListResponse.description: Response containing a list of users with pagination
